body.title: EPIC: Migrate infrastructure modules from Igor to utility closet
body.result: Epic ~90% complete. Phase 1-3 fully shipped (schema-namespaces, base-class-extract, rack-architecture, comms-module, db-proxy-shelf, system-proxy-shelf, channel-tabs-redesign, comms-default-channels). Phase 4-5 mostly shipped (discord-transport, metrics_store, matter-shelf, inference-channel today). Remaining two migrations filed as concrete children: T-uc-budget-shelf and T-uc-filesystem-shelf. Closing epic to prevent catch-all bloat.
body.description:
Sequence the migration of infrastructure modules from wild_igor/igor/ to lab/utility_closet/. Each module becomes a rack shelf.

## Principle
- Conversations → route through comms://
- Service calls → direct call, log audit trail to comms channel
- Moving infra out of Igor reduces cognitive noise in his operating environment

## Migration order (dependencies flow downward)

### Phase 1: Foundation
1. T-uc-schema-three-namespaces — three Postgres schemas
2. T-uc-base-class-extract — AgentBase + AgentClient + ToolRegistry move
3. T-uc-rack-architecture — module registration interface

### Phase 2: Core services
4. T-uc-comms-module — channel routing, envelopes, transports
5. T-uc-db-proxy-shelf — db_proxy as rack shelf + audit logging
6. T-uc-system-proxy-shelf — system_proxy as rack shelf

### Phase 3: Wiring
7. T-uc-comms-default-channels — Shared + per-agent + web UI
8. T-uc-channel-tabs-redesign — web UI with channel tabs

### Phase 4: I/O migration
9. T-uc-budget-shelf — budget tracking as rack shelf (includes SQLite→Postgres)
10. T-uc-discord-transport — Discord bot as comms transport
11. T-uc-metrics-shelf — system metrics as rack shelf

### Phase 5: Advanced
12. T-uc-inference-channel — inference as comms://model/*
13. T-uc-matter-shelf — home automation as rack shelf (GH#185)

## Per-module migration checklist
For each module moved:
- [ ] Create lab/utility_closet/<module>/ directory
- [ ] Move code, update imports
- [ ] Register as UC rack module (health endpoint, standard interface)
- [ ] Add comms://log/<module> channel for audit logging
- [ ] Igor imports from UC shelf (thin facade in igor/ if needed)
- [ ] Tests pass at old AND new import paths during transition
- [ ] Update memory palace pointers

## What stays in Igor (cognition only)
- brainstem/ — core loop, inhibition, basal ganglia
- cognition/ — TWM, attractors, reasoners, turn pipeline, NE, experiment scheduler
- memory/ — cortex, models (but db_proxy moves out)
- training/ — learning, corpus
- tools/ that are engram-native (research cascade, boredom_idle, self_edit)

## What moves (infrastructure/I/O)
- network/system_proxy.py → lab/utility_closet/system_proxy/
- network/network_proxy.py → merged into system_proxy shelf
- network/channels/discord.py → lab/utility_closet/transports/discord.py
- network/channels/direct_url.py → lab/utility_closet/transports/direct_url.py
- tools/budget.py → lab/utility_closet/budget/
- tools/metrics.py → lab/utility_closet/metrics/
- tools/filesystem.py → lab/utility_closet/filesystem/
- memory/db_proxy.py → lab/utility_closet/db_proxy/
- web/ → lab/utility_closet/agent_client.py (already in base class ticket)
- cognition/machine_manager.py → lab/utility_closet/machines/
- cognition/inference_gateway.py → lab/utility_closet/inference/ (Phase 5)

## Success criteria
- Igor's wild_igor/igor/ contains ONLY cognition code
- All infrastructure accessible via UC rack modules
- Igor connects to UC on boot, uses services through standard interfaces
- Each rack shelf independently testable
- comms:// audit trail for all service operations
